question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft an EVM address whose first 4 bytes and next 8 bytes are all zero (e.g., 0x000000000000000000000000<num>) so that EntityServiceImpl redirects the lookup to findById(buffer.getLong()) instead of findByEvmAddress(), allowing the attacker to resolve an arbitrary entity ID and tamper with the perceived identity of a submitted transaction?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user submit a specially crafted EVM address with a zero-padded prefix (0x000000000000000000000000XXXXXXXX) to force the ByteBuffer path in EntityServiceImpl to call findById() with an attacker-controlled long value, causing the service to return a different account entity than the one actually associated with the EVM address in a submitted transaction?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the ByteBuffer.wrap(evmAddressBytes) path where buffer.getInt()==0 && buffer.getLong()==0 to redirect entity resolution to findById(buffer.getLong()), effectively bypassing the evm_address column lookup and returning an entity whose type filter passes, thereby misrepresenting the counterparty in a submitted transaction?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user provide an EVM address of exactly 20 bytes where bytes 0-3 are 0x00000000 and bytes 4-11 are 0x0000000000000000, causing the service to call findById() with the last 8 bytes interpreted as a long, and thereby retrieve an entity that was never associated with that EVM address in any submitted transaction?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user submit an EVM address shorter or longer than 20 bytes (after hex decoding) to cause the ByteBuffer to read incorrect offsets for getInt() and getLong(), producing an unintended entity ID lookup that misrepresents a submitted transaction's participant?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user omit the '0x' prefix from an EVM address input to bypass the Strings.CS.removeStart() stripping in decodeEvmAddress(), causing Hex.decodeHex() to interpret the raw string differently and resolve to a different entity, tampering with the transaction entity lookup result?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EVM address with mixed-case hex characters (e.g., 0xAbCd...) to exploit any case-sensitivity inconsistency in Hex.decodeHex(), causing the byte array to differ from the stored evm_address column value and returning empty or wrong entity data for a submitted transaction?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user pass a null evmAddress to getByEvmAddressAndType(), causing decodeEvmAddress() to return ArrayUtils.EMPTY_BYTE_ARRAY, and then ByteBuffer.wrap() on an empty array to throw a BufferUnderflowException when getInt() is called, resulting in an unhandled exception that leaks internal stack trace information about transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EVM address that decodes to fewer than 12 bytes, causing ByteBuffer.getInt() or ByteBuffer.getLong() to throw a BufferUnderflowException that is not caught by CustomExceptionResolver, leaking internal error details about the transaction entity resolution path?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user craft an EVM address that decodes to exactly 12 bytes (instead of 20), causing buffer.getLong() at position 12 to throw BufferUnderflowException since only 8 bytes remain after reading 4+8=12 bytes, resulting in a denial-of-service or information disclosure for transaction entity lookups?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged user submit a malformed Base32 string as an alias to getByAliasAndType(), causing BASE32.decode() to silently return an incorrect byte array (Apache Commons Codec Base32 is lenient), which then matches a different entity's alias in the database, returning wrong entity data for a submitted transaction?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an alias with padding characters or non-standard Base32 characters that Apache Commons Codec's Base32.decode() silently ignores, producing a byte array that collides with a legitimate entity's alias and returns that entity's data, misrepresenting a submitted transaction's account?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged user pass an empty string as alias to getByAliasAndType(), causing decodeBase32('') to return an empty byte array, which then queries findByAlias(new byte[0]) and potentially matches entities with a null or empty alias column, returning unintended entity data for a submitted transaction?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an extremely long Base32-encoded alias string to getByAliasAndType(), causing decodeBase32() to produce a very large byte array that is passed to the native SQL query findByAlias(), potentially triggering a database error or excessive memory allocation that disrupts transaction entity resolution?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged user craft a Base32 alias that decodes to a byte sequence matching another account's alias, causing getByAliasAndType() to return that account's entity and misrepresent the sender or receiver in a submitted transaction query?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EntityId with shard=0, realm=0, num=0 (EMPTY EntityId) to getByIdAndType(), causing findById(0) to return an unexpected entity or null, and the type filter to pass if the entity type matches, returning incorrect data about a submitted transaction?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EntityId whose encoded long value collides with a different shard/realm/num combination due to the bit-packing scheme in EntityId.encode(), causing getByIdAndType() to return an entity from a different shard or realm and misrepresent a submitted transaction's participant?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EntityId with num at the maximum boundary (274877906943) to getByIdAndType(), causing findById() to query for an entity at the edge of the valid range, and if the type filter passes, return data about a system-level entity involved in submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply a negative entity ID value to getByIdAndType() (if input validation is absent upstream), causing findById() to query with a negative long, potentially matching an entity stored with an unexpected ID and returning wrong transaction participant data?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EntityId where shard=0 and realm=0 so that encode() returns just num (per the special case at line 80-81 of EntityId.java), and then supply a num that matches a high-value system entity ID, causing getByIdAndType() to return that entity and misrepresent a submitted transaction?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the EntityId cache (Caffeine cache with maximumSize=500000) by flooding it with 500,000 unique entity ID lookups via getByIdAndType(), causing cache eviction of legitimate entries and forcing expensive re-lookups that degrade transaction entity resolution performance?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user repeatedly call getByIdAndType() with the same EntityId to exploit the Caffeine cache's expireAfterAccess=60m policy, keeping a stale entity entry alive in the cache for up to 60 minutes after the entity was modified by a submitted transaction, causing stale data to be returned?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EVM address with a '0X' (uppercase X) prefix instead of '0x', bypassing the Strings.CS.removeStart(evmAddress, HEX_PREFIX) stripping (which only removes lowercase '0x'), causing Hex.decodeHex() to fail or produce incorrect bytes that misrepresent a transaction's EVM address?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EVM address with an odd number of hex characters (after stripping '0x') to cause Hex.decodeHex() to throw a DecoderException, which is caught and rethrown as IllegalArgumentException, and then check whether the error message in CustomExceptionResolver leaks the raw evmAddress value back to the user, enabling information disclosure about transaction address formats?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EVM address that is exactly 20 bytes but has bytes 0-3 as 0x00000000 and bytes 4-11 as non-zero, causing the ByteBuffer check to fall through to findByEvmAddress() instead of findById(), and then verify whether this path correctly enforces the deleted is not true filter while the findById() path does not, allowing retrieval of deleted entities?
